gates.requirement: Wrapper metadata declares packageable status, provider API, local adapter API, module entrypoint, server entrypoint, delegated CLI command, and supported tools.
Package metadata has a stable name, version, description, entrypoint, test command, and no private local paths.
Every supported MCP tool delegates to the CLI core instead of reimplementing operation behavior.
Tool input schemas are generated from operation schemas or reviewed against them with any drift documented.
Structured MCP results preserve CLI JSON payloads, including errors, warnings, side effects, approval metadata, and mutation flags.
Apply-capable tools preserve preview-first behavior, approval checks, capability checks, dirty-state blocks, stale-approval blocks, and post-write verification results.
Wrapper tests cover metadata, read-only delegation, stateful adapter-backed delegation, preview/apply lifecycle behavior, command failures, unknown tools, and local-only fragments.
Release gates include MCP wrapper regression and promotion-gate validation alongside CLI, schema, lifecycle, command-adapter, and OpenClaw wrapper checks.
Distribution handoff metadata lists the wrapper promotion gate manifest without moving Portage-owned package, doctor, install, rollback, or release mechanics into Buffet.
Local deployments can use explicit adapter config or LOBSTER_BUFFET_ADAPTER_CONFIG without embedding private adapter data in shared metadata.
